## Key Reasons:
- **High relative cost**: Plans average 21–22% of laptop price (~$100–$130 for your budget), but some exceed 100% of the device cost; one-year accidental plans can be as low as 2% but rarely justify the odds[1].
- **Manufacturer parts are pricey but warranties profit-driven**: Laptop repairs (screen/motherboard) cost $200–$500, yet warranties have low payout rates as "the house always wins"—claims denied more than approved[3][7].
- **Better alternatives exist**:
  - Credit cards often extend warranties free[4].
  - Self-insure: Save the $100–$150 in an emergency fund[4].
  - Retailers/manufacturers may add free extra coverage[4].
- **Data shows low value**: Consumer Reports found median AppleCare ($177) > median repair ($165); same logic applies to budget Windows laptops[5].

**Skip it** unless you're extremely accident-prone (e.g., frequent drops needing "no-fault" coverage)[3]. For your recommended models (ASUS Vivobook, Surface Go, etc.), the standard 1-year warranty suffices for light medical school use[2][3].
